summary.en: A historic fishing quarter turned vibrant beach neighborhood, offering sandy shores, excellent seafood restaurants, and a relaxed maritime atmosphere.
Known as the "Modernist Quarter," Eixample is defined by a brilliant 19th-century urban plan featuring a strict grid of octagonal superblocks designed to maximize sunlight and airflow.
A chic and historic quarter blending medieval charm with modern boutiques, tapas bars, and a vibrant artistic scene centered around the Picasso Museum.
Nestled within the Old City, El Born features winding medieval streets, seamlessly blending ancient history with a relaxed atmosphere of creative craftsmen and trendy boutiques.
Historically the gritty underbelly of the Old City, El Raval has transformed into a multicultural, edgy, and highly artistic hub for the city's contemporary art scene.
Barcelona's oldest district, a maze of narrow, winding streets revealing centuries of history, from Roman ruins to grand Gothic architecture.
Originally an independent town, Gràcia fiercely maintains its "village" ambiance with narrow streets, leafy squares, and a young, cosmopolitan atmosphere devoid of heavy tourist crowds.
Nestled at the foot of Montjuïc mountain, this residential neighborhood is famous for its steep streets and a vibrant, unpretentious local tapas and pintxos culture.
A vibrant, authentic area known for its traditional vermuterias, innovative tapas bars, and a bustling local market, popular with Barcelona residents for its culinary scene.
This former industrial heartland is transforming into a sleek tech hub, yet it retains a quiet, village-like charm and access to less crowded beaches.
A buzzing, authentic neighborhood centered around its magnificent market, known for its vibrant brunch scene, trendy independent shops, and a strong local, community-driven charm.

description.en: Barcelona's most famous city beach, popular for sunbathing, swimming, and beach sports, with a lively promenade.
A lively, authentic neighborhood bar in Barceloneta, popular with locals for its affordable drinks and classic vermouth.
A no-frills, bustling seafood institution in Barceloneta, serving fresh, fried fish and seafood at local prices.
A legendary, family-run restaurant credited with inventing "la bomba" (a fried potato ball), serving traditional seafood tapas since 1944.
Barcelona's historic old port, now a vibrant leisure area with yachts, a marina, and a waterfront promenade.
A historic clock tower that once served as a lighthouse, marking the entrance to the old port.
A popular chiringuito (beach bar) directly on Barceloneta beach, offering drinks and food with ocean views.
An iconic sail-shaped hotel dominating the Barcelona skyline, offering stunning views and a modern architectural landmark.
A casual Italian street-food spot specializing in handmade meatballs and panini, serving bold, quick flavors over a counter with relaxed sidewalk seating.
A spectacular medieval-style castle dropped into the middle of the modernist grid. This Puig i Cadafalch building is uniquely imposing and often overlooked.
A phenomenal local 'bistronomy' spot with Michelin-level culinary creativity without the stiff, formal atmosphere. It’s where local chefs eat on their days off.
A truly bizarre bar decorated like an antique traveling carnival. It's quirky, weird, and provides a completely unique drinking atmosphere.
An independent coffee roaster serving flawless flat whites in a sleek, modern space that perfectly contrasts the old neighborhood.
A hidden, cobblestone pedestrian passage lined with English-style terraced houses that completely breaks the rules of the strict Eixample grid.
A hidden courtyard functioning as an urban beach and pool, it perfectly demonstrates the original 19th-century intent to put an 'inner garden' inside every block.

name.en: Torre de les Aigües (Water Tower of the Eixample)

description.en: A historic Barcelona institution since 1836, famous for its paella and traditional Catalan and Spanish dishes.
A unique "cocktail laboratory" known for its custom-built distillation equipment and innovative drinks.

name.zh-Hans: 斯特拉文斯基医生酒吧
name.zh-Hant: 斯特拉文斯基醫生酒吧

description.en: A cultural center housed in a former market building, preserving the archaeological remains of medieval Barcelona.

name.zh-Hans: 埃尔博恩文化和记忆中心
name.zh-Hant: 埃爾博恩文化和記憶中心
name.en: El Born Centre de Cultura i Memòria

description.en: One of Barcelona's most beautiful and historic railway stations, known for its elegant Art Deco and Art Nouveau architecture.
A serene, hidden Renaissance courtyard that was part of the palace of the viceroys, which most tourists walk right past.
A tucked-away tapas joint that's a favorite among local chefs, where traditional dishes are executed with modern, high-quality techniques.
A legendary local bakery and pastry shop where the chocolate cakes are considered edible works of modern art.
A renowned wine bar situated in a historic square, offering an incredible array of Spanish wines and local snacks with an unbeatable view of Santa Maria del Mar.
A beautifully restored iron-and-glass market that sits directly over the excavated ruins of the 1700s city, offering secret history beneath your feet.

name.en: Mercat del Born (Born Centre de Cultura i Memòria)

description.en: Tucked down a hidden, pedestrian-only alley, this shop is widely considered to have the best specialty coffee roasters in all of Spain.
A breathtaking Gothic basilica built by the local working class, it features an architectural purity that rivals the main Cathedral.
An elegant wine bar overlooking the Santa Maria del Mar Basilica, offering a curated selection of wines by the glass.
Home to one of the most extensive collections of artworks by the 20th-century Spanish artist Pablo Picasso.
A breathtaking example of Catalan Gothic architecture, built by and for the people of the Ribera district.
A popular spot offering creative and refined tapas dishes in a lively, modern setting.
A lively and highly-regarded traditional tapas bar known for its fresh seafood and bustling, authentic atmosphere.
A lively street known for its vibrant street art, trendy bars, and independent shops, showcasing Raval's edgy side.
A multidisciplinary cultural center focusing on urban culture and contemporary issues, with a distinctive glass facade.

name.zh-Hans: 巴塞罗那当代文化中心
name.zh-Hant: 巴塞隆納當代文化中心

description.en: An iconic independent record shop on Carrer dels Tallers, this is a beloved staple of Barcelona's alternative and underground music scene.
An Asian-tapas fusion spot founded by a former El Bulli chef, offering Michelin-level culinary creativity in a completely informal setting.
A historic dairy cafe operating since 1870, it is the actual birthplace of the famous Spanish chocolate drink, Cacaolat.
A hauntingly quiet 15th-century gothic courtyard filled with orange trees. It was formerly a medieval hospital and is the location where Antoni Gaudí tragically died.

name.en: Hospital de la Santa Creu (Old Courtyard)

description.en: A former modernist candy shop from 1912 turned into a cocktail bar. The impeccably preserved interior feels like stepping into a time machine.
A cool bar associated with the Filmoteca de Catalunya, offering drinks and light bites in a cinematic atmosphere.
Barcelona's contemporary art museum, housed in a striking modern building, a hub for skate culture outside.

name.zh-Hans: 巴塞罗那当代艺术博物馆
name.zh-Hant: 巴塞隆納當代藝術博物館

description.en: Barcelona's oldest bar, famous for its absinthe ritual, frequented by artists like Picasso and Hemingway.
The stark white square outside the Contemporary Art Museum of Barcelona (MACBA) is the undisputed epicenter of Barcelona's world-famous street skating scene.
A wide, tree-lined boulevard in the heart of Raval, famous for Botero's 'El Gato' statue and diverse street life.
A highly-rated Moroccan restaurant that perfectly reflects the authentic, vibrant North African multiculturalism of the neighborhood.
A popular spot for innovative and traditional tapas, known for its vibrant atmosphere and quality ingredients.
A magnificent Gothic cathedral dedicated to Saint Eulalia, with a stunning cloister home to 13 white geese.
Established in 1786, this is the oldest restaurant in Barcelona, serving classic Catalan cuisine in a historic setting.
A unique, enchanting bar designed to look like a magical forest, complete with trees, waterfalls, and fairy figures.
Delve beneath Plaça del Rei to discover extensive Roman ruins, a fascinating archaeological journey through Barcelona's past.

name.zh-Hans: 巴塞罗那历史博物馆雷伊广场
name.zh-Hant: 巴塞隆納歷史博物館雷伊廣場

description.en: An award-winning speakeasy cocktail bar hidden behind a 'pastrami bar' freezer door, known for its innovative drinks.
A beautiful, tranquil square scarred by civil war bombings, offering a poignant and peaceful escape from the bustling streets.
Four towering Corinthian columns, all that remains of an ancient Roman temple, tucked away inside a modern building courtyard.
Bustling streets renowned for their array of independent boutiques, quirky shops, and local designer stores.

name.zh-Hans: 阿斯图里亚斯街和威尔第街
name.zh-Hant: 阿斯圖里亞斯街和威爾第街

description.en: Antoni Gaudí's first major architectural masterpiece. This UNESCO site is an incredible display of Moorish influence and is far less crowded than his other works.
A Michelin-starred restaurant offering creative and exquisite tasting menus with a focus on seasonal, local ingredients.
A classic tavern that serves traditional Catalan meals, offering highly authentic regional dishes at very reasonable prices.
A popular and highly-rated restaurant offering contemporary Catalan dishes with a focus on fresh, seasonal produce.
A tranquil, relatively hidden green space offering a peaceful escape, often featuring art installations or local events.

name.en: Jardins de la Vila de Gràcia

description.en: A charming and cozy chocolateria famous for its thick, rich hot chocolate and churros, perfect for a sweet treat.
An informal, vibrant seafood spot that offers a modern, unfussy take on fresh grilled seafood, avoiding typical tourist clichés.
A cozy, stylish bodega serving excellent local vermouth and craft beer. It's the quintessential neighborhood watering hole for an authentic pre-dinner drink.
A sophisticated bar specializing in classic cocktails and an extensive gin and tonic menu in an elegant setting.

name.zh-Hans: 老式金汤力鸡尾酒酒吧
name.zh-Hant: 老式金湯力雞尾酒酒吧
name.en: Old Fashioned Gin Tonic & Cocktail Bar

description.en: The central square of Gràcia, dominated by its iconic clock tower and surrounded by charming architecture.

name.en: Plaça de la Vila de Gràcia

description.en: A leafy, quiet square anchored by the Sant Joan church. It's the absolute best place to soak in the authentic 'village' feel of Gràcia.
A tiny, independent specialty coffee pioneer beloved by local residents for its true third-wave coffee.
One of Barcelona's most important venues for live performing arts, this theater is the beating heart of the neighborhood's alternative and cultural scene.
A boutique wine bar that merges a romantic atmosphere with top-tier local gastronomy, pairing an extensive list of Spanish wines with exquisite cheeses.
A sustainable, slow-food cafe that sits perfectly at the base of the Montjuïc trails, offering locally brewed craft beers and organic snacks.
A quirky, eclectic neighborhood bodega packed with bizarre antiques where locals drink vermouth directly from massive wooden barrels.
A pedestrian street lined wall-to-wall with tiny pintxo bars. It's the ultimate cheap bar-hopping experience where locals grab €1.50 snacks on sticks.
A massive cactus garden on the slopes of Montjuïc. It's one of the largest cactus collections in Europe, extremely photogenic and rarely crowded.

name.en: Jardins de Mossèn Costa i Llobera

description.en: A legendary, standing-room-only family tapas bar famous for serving the best 'conservas' (high-end canned seafood) and 'montaditos' in the city.
A preserved underground bomb shelter that offers a gripping, rarely-visited piece of secret history documenting Barcelona's resilience during the Spanish Civil War.
A highly-rated restaurant offering modern Galician cuisine with a creative twist, particularly known for its rice dishes.
A hugely popular bar in Sant Antoni, famous for its vermouth, craft beers, and lively outdoor terrace.
A classic, unpretentious bodega offering traditional vermouth, local wines, and simple tapas in an authentic setting.
The main artery of Sant Antoni, famous for its concentration of trendy bars, cafes, and restaurants, perfect for a stroll.
A bustling wine bar and bodega with an extensive selection of wines by the glass, specializing in cold cuts, cheeses, and traditional tapas.
An urban park featuring three distinctive chimneys, known for its skate park and a vibrant display of street art.
A beautifully restored iron-and-glass market building offering fresh produce, clothes, and a weekly Sunday book and coin fair.
A leading contemporary theatre known for its innovative productions, located at the foot of Montjuïc.
An open-air amphitheater built directly into the mountain rock. Though built for the 1929 Expo, it feels like stumbling upon ancient Greek ruins.
A former neighborhood hardware store transformed into a mid-century cocktail bar, featuring incredible retro signage and phenomenal, locally sourced vermouth.
A wildly popular, tiny independent brunch and street-food spot that has become a beloved local staple for modern urban comfort food.
A contemporary Catalan art museum housed in a beautifully restored factory, showcasing a masterful architectural blend of crumbling industrial brick and stark modern concrete.
Originally a humble fisherman's tavern, it now serves impeccable seafood in a quiet, tree-lined square far from the chaotic tourist traps.
A historic cemetery featuring incredible neoclassical sculptures. It's a hauntingly beautiful, quiet open-air museum of macabre art away from the crowds.
The neighborhood's main pedestrian artery, it offers all the charm, trees, and outdoor seating of Las Ramblas but with virtually none of the tourist traps.
A specialty cafe operating out of a vintage 1972 Citroën van parked inside a massive warehouse, offering the ultimate industrial-chic design experience.
One of Antoni Gaudí's most iconic buildings, famous for its skeletal facade and 'dragon-back' roof design.
Barcelona's iconic and famously unfinished church, a masterpiece of Gaudí's architectural vision, with construction ongoing for over a century.
A popular local spot for high-quality tapas where daily specials allow you to explore new flavors with seasonal products.
A unique cafe offering a curated collection of sweets, treats, and drinks made by monks and nuns from various monasteries.
A tiny, classic Old Town bar that is constantly packed with locals and serves only four traditional items on its menu.
A shrine to sweets where you can gaze upon intricate chocolate sculptures depicting everything from famous landmarks to historical scenes.
A beautiful but toxic fountain of pure mercury, designed by Alexander Calder and housed safely behind glass at the Fundació Joan Miró.
A sprawling park and garden where Gaudí's famously flamboyant and colorful architectural style is on full display.
A serene go-to spot for locals craving perfectly prepared paella and fish dishes with the Mediterranean Sea as a backdrop.
An always-crowded, good-energy tapas bar that is a staple of the Sant Antoni neighborhood.
A historic cemetery on Montjuïc hill, known for its impressive collection of modernist statues and ornate tombs.
A restaurant by Chef Jordi Esteve featuring modern and imaginative Catalan cuisine that blends tradition with a contemporary touch.
A small, modern bar that lives and breathes the local 'vermut' tradition, offering a truly authentic vibe.
A beautiful and classic Art Deco bar serving traditional Catalan specialties in an elegant two-story space.
Considered one of Barcelona's best restaurants for high-quality meat dishes and steaks.
An immersive experience with mind-bending exhibits and optical illusions that challenge your perception of reality.
A quintessential tiny nook with a bar that fits only two people, serving authentic and beloved sandwiches.
One of Barcelona's oldest waterworks, this historic fountain dates back to 1375 and is brimming with history.
A cultural melting pot with galleries and stages, offering creative workshops, theater, and film clubs.
An old literary haunt transformed into a full-on seafood bar by the team behind the famous Paradiso cocktail bar.
The trendsetting spot that started the natural wine and small plates movement in Barcelona, with a lively atmosphere.
Located within the Parc de la Ciutadella, this city zoo is home to a wide variety of animal species from around the world.
A stylish Middle Eastern eatery in Poblenou that is perfect for a special occasion dinner.
A cozy wine bar with a seasonal menu and a funky selection of natural wines, perfect for an evening drink.
The ideal place to 'vermutear' (have a vermouth). Come for the classic aperitif and stick around for an excellent dinner.
A loud, boisterous, and authentic bar beloved by locals for a casual mid-week dinner near the sea.
Located in Time Out Market, this world-class barbecue restaurant ensures every dish has a touch of fire.

prompt.en: Take a messy, drool-worthy cross-section shot of a handmade meatball panino.
Capture the relaxed atmosphere of the sidewalk seating area, with your Italian street food in the foreground.
Get a low-angle shot where the pointed spires ('punxes') of the building converge sharply against the sky.
Capture a detail of the building's facade that shows its unique medieval-style architecture within the modernist Eixample grid.
Photograph the stunning plating of one of their signature dishes from directly overhead.
Take a photo of the restaurant's understated exterior, capturing the vibe of a place where local chefs eat on their days off.
Find the vintage funhouse mirrors and capture a distorted, quirky selfie of you or your group.
Find the most bizarre piece of antique carnival decor in the bar and take a creative photo of it.
Photograph the gleaming modern espresso machine with the historic Eixample street grid visible through the window.
Capture a top-down view of a flawless flat white, focusing on the perfect latte art.
Capture the symmetrical line of vintage streetlamps running down the center of the quiet, cobblestone passage.
Photograph the English-style terraced houses, highlighting how they break the architectural rules of the surrounding neighborhood.
Photograph the old brick water tower perfectly reflected in the shallow courtyard water of the urban beach.
Capture the scene of people enjoying the hidden urban beach, showcasing the concept of an 'inner garden' in the city block.
Find the intricately carved wooden ceiling of the main staircase and photograph it looking straight up.
Take a perfectly symmetrical photo of the serene Renaissance courtyard, capturing its quiet, hidden atmosphere.
Use a slow shutter speed to capture the blurring, bustling energy of the waiters moving through the narrow room.
Take a close-up, appetizing shot of your favorite modern tapa from this chef-approved spot.
Take a macro shot of the glossy, mirror-like glaze on their signature chocolate cake.
Photograph the artistic display of various pastries in the shop's window.
Photograph your glass of wine on a terrace table with the imposing gothic church looming in the background.
Capture the incredible array of Spanish wine bottles displayed inside the bar.
Look down from the interior balconies to capture the contrast between the 19th-century ironwork and the 18th-century stone ruins below.
From the outside, take a wide shot that captures the grand scale of the restored iron-and-glass market structure.
Capture the neon 'Nomad' sign glowing against the dark stone of the ancient alleyway.
Photograph the minimalist, industrial interior of the coffee lab, showing where the magic happens.
Stand in the absolute center of the aisle and shoot a perfectly symmetrical photo of the towering nave columns.
Capture the light streaming through the basilica's massive stained glass windows, creating patterns on the stone floor.
Photograph the densely packed vinyl racks, creating a leading line toward the neon signs at the back of the store.
Capture the store's iconic exterior and signage on Carrer dels Tallers, a staple of the alternative scene.
Capture the glowing red lanterns contrasting with the sleek, minimalist bar.
Take an artful close-up of one of the creative Asian-tapas dishes, focusing on the plating.
Take a top-down shot of your hot chocolate or Cacaolat against the vintage tiled floor and classic marble tables.
Photograph the historic facade of the cafe, which has been operating since 1870.
Find the gothic stone arches and use them to naturally frame the courtyard's central cross.
Capture the haunting contrast between the bright orange trees and the ancient, weathered gothic stone of the courtyard.
Photograph the antique glass cabinets that now house liquor bottles instead of sweets.
Capture a classic cocktail on the bar, with the ornate, time-capsule interior reflected on the glass.
Freeze a skateboarder mid-air doing a trick, using the stark white museum wall as a clean, minimalist backdrop.
Take a wide, architectural shot of the stark white MACBA facade, emphasizing its geometric lines.
Create a vibrant, colorful photo of a traditional tagine alongside a glass of pouring mint tea.
Capture the intricate details of the Moroccan lamps, textiles, or decor inside the restaurant.
Capture a photo highlighting the reflection of the city or sky on the distinctive glass facade of CCCB.
Shoot the vibrant green and white exterior tiles framed tightly by the wrought-iron palm leaf gates.
Find a unique angle that highlights the incredible Moorish influence on the building's facade.
Capture the rustic interior, specifically the hanging cured meats suspended over the dining room.
Take a photo of a traditional Catalan dish on your table that showcases the authentic, rustic feel of the tavern.
Get an action shot of the open grill flames in the kitchen as the chefs prepare fresh seafood.
Photograph your plate of fresh grilled seafood, capturing the modern, unfussy presentation.
Capture a close-up of the antique wooden wine barrels behind the bar with a shallow depth of field.
Photograph your glass of local vermouth, capturing the cozy, stylish ambiance of this quintessential neighborhood bodega.
Take a low-angle shot of the Sant Joan church tower bathed in golden hour light.
Capture the authentic 'village' feel of the square, with locals relaxing on benches or at cafe tables.
Find the shop's minimalist wood facade and photograph it perfectly framed between passing locals.
Get a close-up shot of your specialty coffee, focusing on the latte art or the rich color of the brew.
Find and photograph the theater's modern facade right at twilight, balancing the artificial lights with the blue sky.
Capture the energy of the theater's entrance as the audience arrives for a performance.
Take a top-down macro shot of the cheese platter, highlighting the diverse textures of the exquisite cheeses.
Capture the romantic atmosphere by photographing a glass of Spanish wine perfectly paired with a piece of cheese.
Photograph the rustic facade and the vintage bicycles often parked out front.
Capture your craft beer or organic snack with the greenery of the nearby Montjuïc trails in the background.
Aim your camera at the chaotic, cluttered ceiling adorned with old instruments, bizarre antiques, and other oddities.
Take a photo of the massive wooden barrels from which the bar serves its local vermouth.
Create a colorful, close-up composition of the various toothpicks collected from your eaten pintxos.
Take a photo of a bar counter overflowing with the colorful and diverse selection of pintxos.
Silhouette a towering, surreal cactus against the bright blue backdrop of the Mediterranean Sea.
Take a macro shot that shows off the unique, intricate patterns and textures of a cactus or succulent.
Capture the towering, floor-to-ceiling shelves of rare liquor and wine bottles behind the tiny bar.
Get a delicious-looking close-up of one of the bar's famous 'montaditos' (small open-faced sandwiches).
Photograph the long, dimly lit tunnel of the bomb shelter as it converges into absolute darkness.
Capture the unassuming entrance to the shelter from the street, hinting at the secret history hidden below.
Take a perfectly symmetrical shot from the absolute top tier of the stone seats looking down at the stage.
Stand on the stage and take a low-angle shot looking up at the towering stone amphitheater seats.
Capture the glowing red neon 'Balius' hardware sign illuminating the street at twilight.
Photograph your cocktail on the bar, making sure to include elements of the bar's retro, mid-century decor.
Snap the vibrant, contrasting colors of their bright pink lemonade next to a stack of pancakes or other brunch item.
Photograph the queue of locals waiting outside, a testament to this tiny spot's popularity.
Photograph the stark contrast of pristine white contemporary artwork hung against the raw, exposed brick interior walls.
Capture the museum's exterior, focusing on the architectural blend of the old factory building and modern concrete additions.
Capture the tranquil, leafy terrace setting in Plaça Prim before your food arrives.
Take an appetizing photo of an impeccably prepared seafood dish, reflecting the restaurant's origins as a fisherman's tavern.
Seek out the chilling 'Kiss of Death' sculpture and frame your shot tightly on the skeleton's wings and haunting embrace.
Take a long shot down one of the main avenues, capturing the rows of ornate tombs and neoclassical sculptures.
Capture the dense canopy of trees stretching all the way down the boulevard, creating a tunnel that leads toward the Mediterranean Sea.
Photograph people enjoying the outdoor seating at a cafe, capturing the relaxed, non-touristy charm of this local artery.
Capture the silver vintage Citroën van gleaming inside the vast, minimalist warehouse space.
Place your coffee cup on an industrial-chic table or surface and take a photo that captures the warehouse aesthetic.
Find an ornate balcony, doorway, or lamp post and capture its most intricate detail. Look for flowing lines and nature-inspired motifs.
Take a selfie at a 'chaflán' (the famous octagonal corner of the Eixample grid) with the long, straight streets stretching out behind you.
Many Eixample apartment blocks hide beautiful interior courtyards ('patis interiors'). Peek through an open gate and snap a photo of one of these hidden green spaces.
Find a cafe or restaurant with elegant outdoor seating and take an artistic shot of the setup, focusing on the details.
Capture the upscale atmosphere of Passeig de Gràcia by photographing a designer storefront or a stylish local.
Use the neighborhood's grid layout to your advantage. Find a spot in the middle of a street and take a perfectly symmetrical photo looking down the road.
Find a puddle or a shop window and capture the reflection of a grand Modernista building.
Eixample is full of great coffee shops. Take a picture of your 'café con leche' or espresso from a local spot.
Motorbikes and scooters are everywhere. Find a row of parked scooters and take a cool, colorful photo.
Look for old shop signs or building names with classic Art Nouveau lettering and capture a close-up.
The tree-lined streets are a key feature. Take a photo looking up through the canopy of plane trees that line the avenues.
On a sunny day, capture the long shadows cast by the buildings and trees across the wide sidewalks.
Find a massive, ornate wooden door on one of the residential buildings and photograph its impressive scale and details.
Capture a classic Barcelona taxi (black and yellow) in motion against the backdrop of the neighborhood's architecture.
Take a photo that you feel perfectly captures the elegant and orderly atmosphere of Eixample.
Find the narrowest, most atmospheric alleyway you can and take a photo looking up at the sliver of sky between the old buildings.
Photograph a detail that looks like it hasn't changed in 500 years—a worn stone step, an iron ring on a wall, or a faded coat of arms.
Stumble upon a quiet, hidden square (plaça) and take a selfie with the fountain or ancient walls in the background.
Look up! Find and photograph a gargoyle or stone carving on one of the old churches or buildings.
Find a section of the ancient Roman wall and take a photo that shows its scale and age.
Capture a scene that shows a stark contrast between the ancient neighborhood and modern life, like a street artist next to a medieval wall.
The narrow streets create dramatic shadows. Take a photo that plays with the strong contrast between light and shadow.
There are many small, historic fountains in the Gothic Quarter. Find one and take a creative close-up shot.
Find a traditional, old-fashioned shop sign made of wood, iron, or mosaic and capture its charm.
Take a photo of a quiet corner or empty alley that feels like your own secret discovery in the bustling neighborhood.
Look for laundry hanging from balconies, a classic sight in the old city. Capture this slice of local life.
Find a street performer and capture the atmosphere of their music filling the ancient streets.
Use a stone archway to frame a photo of a street or square beyond it.
Take a low-angle shot focusing on the texture and pattern of the historic cobblestone streets.
After a rain, or with a spilled drink, capture the reflection of the Gothic architecture in a puddle on the street.
Find a unique artisan boutique and capture a creative reflection of the street in its window display.
Take an artistic, mouth-watering photo of a counter full of delicious-looking tapas or pintxos at a local bar.
Find a spot where a medieval archway or narrow street frames a modern shop, piece of street art, or trendy cafe.
Take a photo of yourself strolling down the tree-lined Passeig del Born, capturing the lively atmosphere.
Peer into an artisan's workshop (leather goods, jewelry, etc.) and discreetly capture a photo of their craft.
Get a glass of cava at a classic 'xampanyeria' and take a celebratory photo.
Many of the old palaces on Carrer de Montcada have hidden courtyards. Find an open one and snap a picture of the peaceful space.
Find a particularly colorful building facade, door, or window box and make it the subject of your photo.
Capture the reflection of the sky and surrounding buildings in the glass of the old Mercat del Born.
Take a photo that encapsulates the trendy, historic, and creative energy of El Born for you.
El Born is a fashionable neighborhood. Capture a candid shot of local street style (from a respectful distance).
Find a cozy wine bar and take an atmospheric photo of a glass of wine on the table.
Imagine Picasso walking these streets. Take a photo of an alleyway that looks like it could have inspired one of his early works.
El Born has great pastry shops ('pastisseries'). Photograph a beautiful pastry or chocolate in a shop window.
As evening falls, capture the warm glow of the lights from bars and shops spilling out onto the narrow streets.
Create a colorful flat-lay photo of your plate of pintxos from one of the many bars on Carrer de Blai.
Find a set of public stairs leading up the hill of Montjuïc and capture a photo looking up or down the steep incline.
Take a selfie in front of a classic theater marquee on Avinguda del Paral·lel, the 'Broadway' of Barcelona.
Find a traditional neighborhood bodega with old wine barrels and capture its authentic, timeless atmosphere.
Photograph a quiet residential street away from the main drags, capturing the feeling of a real, lived-in neighborhood.
Capture the energy of Carrer de Blai in the evening, with people spilling out of the pintxos bars onto the street.
Find one of the small parks or gardens at the foot of Montjuïc and take a photo of the lush greenery against the urban backdrop.
Choose the most interesting-looking pintxo you can find and take a heroic close-up shot of it before you eat it.
Photograph an old, traditional building next to a more modern apartment block to show the neighborhood's evolution.
Capture a photo of the iconic Tres Xemeneies (Three Chimneys) of the old power station that mark the edge of the neighborhood.
Find a classic, no-frills corner bar and take a picture of its exterior, capturing the local vibe.
Find a hidden piece of street art or graffiti on a side street and snap a photo.
Take a photo of a glass of 'vermut' with its classic accompaniments like an olive and a slice of orange.
From one of the streets at the base of Montjuïc, take a photo looking up at the cable car or the Palau Nacional.
Take a single photo that you feel best represents the authentic, lively, and unpretentious character of Poble Sec.
Capture a candid moment of daily life in one of Gràcia's charming squares (plaças)—people chatting, kids playing, or someone reading on a bench.
Find the most beautifully decorated balcony with an abundance of plants and flowers and take a picture.
Find a piece of street art or a quirky shop sign that sums up the neighborhood's vibe and take a selfie with it.
Take a photo down a narrow, quiet street that makes Gràcia feel like a separate village within the city.
Photograph the storefront of an independent, locally-owned shop—a bookstore, a record shop, or an artisan food store.
Frame a shot of the clock tower in Plaça de la Vila de Gràcia against the sky or surrounding buildings.
Find an empty bench in a sunny spot in one of the squares and take an inviting photo.
Buy a pastry or a scoop of ice cream from a local shop and take a delicious-looking photo of it.
Many shops have painted shutters. Find a closed shop with interesting graffiti or art on its metal shutter and take a picture.
Capture the exterior of a charming corner bar or cafe, ideally with people enjoying themselves at outdoor tables.
Find the exterior of Casa Vicens, Gaudí's first major project, and capture its unique, colorful tile work.
Many streets in Gràcia are pedestrian-only. Take a photo that emphasizes the car-free, walkable nature of the neighborhood.
Visit a local fruit and vegetable shop and take a colorful photo of the fresh produce on display.
In the late afternoon, capture the long shadows of trees and buildings stretching across one of the plaças.
Take one photo that you feel perfectly captures the unique, bohemian, and community-focused spirit of Gràcia.
Find a scene that shows an old factory chimney or brick warehouse next to a modern glass office building.
Take a photo down the center of the Rambla del Poblenou, capturing the trees, benches, and pedestrian walkway leading towards the sea.
Poblenou is full of large-scale murals. Find your favorite one and strike a pose in front of it.
Walk to the beach at the end of the Rambla and take a photo of the sea and the horizon.
Find a building that was clearly an old factory or warehouse but is now a loft, office, or coffee shop, and photograph its new life.
Explore one of Poblenou's 'superblocks' (superilles) and capture the car-free, pedestrian-friendly environment with its benches and plants.
Poblenou is a hub for specialty coffee. Take an artistic shot of your flat white or pour-over from a local roaster.
Use the modern glass buildings of the 22@ district to capture a reflection of an older, industrial building.
Take a single photo that you feel captures the unique mix of old industry, new technology, art, and beach life in Poblenou.
From outside the historic Poblenou Cemetery, capture a photo of the ornate statues and tombs.
Find a traditional 'orxateria' on the Rambla del Poblenou and take a refreshing photo of a glass of horchata.
Find a bar or cafe on the Rambla filled with locals and capture the relaxed, community atmosphere.
Photograph an interesting architectural detail on one of the modern buildings in the 22@ innovation district.
Find a station for the 'Bicing' public bikes and take a cool photo of the rows of yellow and red bikes.
Take a photo from within the neighborhood looking down a street that ends with a view of the blue Mediterranean Sea.
Capture the stunning modernista architecture and reflective roof of the Mercat de Sant Antoni.
Take a selfie enjoying a glass of vermouth at one of the neighborhood's many trendy or traditional bodegas.
If it's Sunday, capture the atmosphere of the book and collectibles market. If not, find a cool independent bookshop exterior.
Find a spot on the hip Carrer del Parlament and take a photo capturing the stylish people and bustling cafe culture.
Sant Antoni is famous for brunch. Take a mouth-watering photo of a brunch dish from a local cafe.
Take an artistic photo of your coffee cup or vermouth glass on an outdoor table, with the street scene blurred in the background.
Inside the market, find the area where you can look down at the preserved Roman ruins and take a photo of this hidden history.
Take a single photo that you feel best captures the trendy-but-local, foodie-centric atmosphere of Sant Antoni.
Find a traditional neighborhood shop—a bakery, a hardware store—and photograph its classic storefront.
The neighborhood has excellent bakeries. Find a beautiful croissant or pastry and take a close-up shot.
Keep your eyes peeled for small-scale street art, like stickers or small paintings, and capture a detail.
Step off the main foodie streets and take a photo of a quiet, residential block to show the other side of the neighborhood.
Take a colorful photo of a fruit, vegetable, or fish stall inside the Mercat de Sant Antoni.
Capture the motion of cyclists using the neighborhood's well-defined bike lanes.
As evening approaches, capture the lively atmosphere as bars and restaurants fill up with people.
Find a gargoyle or a strange stone face carved into a building and take a picture of it. What stories could it tell?
Stand in the middle of the narrowest alley you can find and take a photo looking straight up at the sliver of sky.
Find a section of the ancient Roman wall and capture the texture of the centuries-old stones.
Discover a hidden plaça (square) and take a photo that captures its unique, quiet charm.
Take a selfie in the reflection of an old, traditional shop window.
Find the most ancient-looking wooden door and photograph its details.
Take an artistic, low-angle shot of the neighborhood's worn cobblestone streets.
Find a 'pastisseria' (pastry shop) and take a mouth-watering photo of the treats in the window.
Capture the intricate patterns of a wrought-iron balcony.
Photograph one of the classic, old-fashioned streetlights that illuminate the Gothic Quarter's nights.
Snap a picture of a glass of vermouth or local wine at a tiny, traditional bar.
Take a creative photo of your own shadow stretching across the narrow, historic streets.
Find an unexpected piece of modern street art contrasting with the ancient walls.
Discover a public fountain, big or small, and capture the movement of the water.
Photograph a vintage or hand-painted shop sign that speaks to the neighborhood's long history.
Find a building with classic Modernista architecture (wavy lines, colorful tiles, ornate ironwork) and take a photo of its most stunning detail.
Stand at one of the iconic chamfered corners (xemfrà) and capture the perfect symmetry of the street grid.
Peek into one of the large doorways to find an 'interior de l'illa' (inner block courtyard) and snap a picture of this hidden green space.
Photograph the most opulent and grand entryway or lobby you can find.
Take a photo of a high-fashion item in a window display on Passeig de Gràcia.
Capture a row of elegant Eixample balconies, focusing on their patterns and decorations.
Take a selfie enjoying a coffee at a chic sidewalk café.
Look up! Take a photo of the geometric patterns created by the buildings against the sky.
Find a line of parked scooters and take an interesting photo of this common Barcelona sight.
Find a colorful or patterned tile floor or wall and take a close-up shot.
Capture the bustling energy of one of the wide, tree-lined boulevards.
Photograph a piece of art through the window of one of Eixample's many art galleries.
Take a picture of a beautifully presented dish or cocktail at an upscale restaurant or bar.
Find an ornate public bench and take a photo that makes it look like a throne.
Capture the reflection of a grand Eixample building in a puddle, a window, or a pair of sunglasses.
Find a chic, independent boutique and photograph a unique item in the window display you'd love to own.
Capture a scene that shows the contrast between El Born's medieval architecture and its modern, trendy vibe.
Photograph the hands of an artisan at work or a display of handmade goods in a local workshop.
Take a selfie strolling down the lively, tree-lined Passeig del Born.
Find a tapas bar and take an artistic, close-up photo of a beautifully arranged tapa or pintxo.
Discover one of El Born's quiet, hidden courtyards and capture its peaceful atmosphere.
Discreetly capture the effortlessly cool style of a local resident or artist in the neighborhood.
Take a picture of a glass of cava, the quintessential drink of the neighborhood.
Find a building with brightly colored window shutters and make them the star of your photo.
Photograph a street sign named after a historic guild or craft, like 'Carrer dels Cotoners' (Cotton-makers' street).
Take a photo that shows the lush greenery of Parc de la Ciutadella bordering the neighborhood.
Capture the exterior of one of the neighborhood's many museums (like Picasso or Moco) in a creative way.
As evening falls, photograph a cool neon sign from one of El Born's many cocktail bars.
Find a stone archway or covered passage and take a photo looking through it to the street beyond.
Take a picture of a gourmet food shop window, focusing on local products like olive oil, cheese, or jamón.
On Carrer de Blai, find the most colorful and appetizing-looking pintxo on a bar counter and take a close-up shot.
Take a photo of the marquee or entrance of one of the many theaters along Avinguda del Paral·lel.
Find a classic, old-school bodega (wine cellar/bar) and capture its authentic, no-frills charm.
Find a set of public stairs leading up towards Montjuïc hill and take a photo from the bottom looking up.
Take a selfie in a quiet, residential square, capturing the everyday life of the neighborhood in the background.
Capture a picture of a glass of 'vermut de la casa' (house vermouth) with its classic olive and orange slice.
Find a small park or garden, like the Jardins de les Tres Xemeneies, and photograph a detail that shows its urban character.
Look up and photograph laundry hanging from the balconies, a classic Barcelona scene.
Find a piece of graffiti or street art that seems to tell a story about the neighborhood.
Take a photo of a lively corner bar with locals enjoying themselves at the outdoor tables.
Find a chalkboard sign advertising the 'Menú del Día' (Menu of the Day) and snap a pic of the delicious offerings.
Photograph a simple, unadorned residential building façade that shows the authentic character of Poble Sec.
Capture a candid photo of a local walking their dog, a common sight in this residential area.
Find a spot where a traditional, old building stands next to a modern one and capture the contrast.
As evening arrives, take a photo of the warm glow from a bar or restaurant spilling onto the street.
Find a lively plaça (square) and take a photo that captures its village-like atmosphere, with people chatting, kids playing, or friends having a drink.
Photograph a balcony overflowing with plants, flags, or quirky decorations that reflect Gràcia's bohemian spirit.
Find an independent, locally-owned shop with a unique name or storefront and capture its charm.
Gràcia's streets are full of art. Find a cool mural or stencil and take a creative selfie with it.
Wander off the main streets and photograph a quiet, picturesque corner that feels like a secret.
Frame a shot of the iconic clock tower in Plaça de la Vila de Gràcia.
Capture the colors and energy of a local food market or a fruit and vegetable stand.
Find a traditional 'orxateria' and take a picture of a refreshing glass of orxata (horchata).
Take a photo down one of Gràcia's narrow, pedestrian-friendly streets, focusing on the vanishing point.
Photograph the vintage facade of an independent cinema, like Cine Verdi.
Find a poster or notice for a local community event and take a picture of it.
Photograph a bicycle parked against a colorful wall, capturing the relaxed mode of transport in the neighborhood.
Gràcia is full of international cuisine. Take a photo of a menu or sign from a restaurant that isn't Spanish or Catalan.
Capture the interesting patterns of shadows cast by trees or buildings on a sunny plaça.
Look for leftover decorations from the famous Festa Major de Gràcia, even if it's not August, and snap a picture.
Find an old factory chimney ('xemeneia') or a piece of industrial architecture and photograph it against the modern skyline.
Poblenou is an open-air gallery. Find a large, impressive mural on the side of a warehouse or building and capture its scale.
Photograph a modern architectural landmark, like the Torre Glòries or the Design Museum.
Take a photo that captures the relaxed, local vibe of the Rambla del Poblenou, with its outdoor cafes and shady trees.
Walk to the end of the Rambla and take a picture of the point where the neighborhood meets the Mediterranean Sea.
Find a building that has clearly been converted from an old warehouse into a modern office, studio, or loft and photograph the contrast.
Take a selfie outside a co-working space or creative agency in the @22 innovation district.
Poblenou is famous for its specialty coffee. Take an artistic shot of a latte or pour-over from a local roaster.
Discover a community garden or a green space tucked between modern buildings and capture this urban oasis.
Find one of Poblenou's 'superilles' (superblocks) and take a photo of the pedestrian-friendly, car-free environment.
Look for a faded, painted advertisement on the side of an old brick building—a 'ghost sign' from its industrial past.
Capture the reflection of an old industrial building in the glass facade of a new tech office.
Take a mouth-watering photo of a brunch dish from one of the neighborhood's many popular brunch spots.
Photograph the ornate entrance or a peaceful corner of the historic Poblenou Cemetery.
Take a dynamic photo of the wide, green bike lanes that are characteristic of the neighborhood.
Capture the stunning architecture of the restored Mercat de Sant Antoni, focusing on its iron structure and modern design.
Find a bustling vermuteria on Carrer del Parlament and take a photo that captures the lively 'fer el vermut' tradition.
On a Sunday, photograph the stalls of the weekly book market outside the Mercat de Sant Antoni.
Take a selfie enjoying a specialty coffee from one of the neighborhood's many trendy, third-wave coffee shops.
Order some tapas and a drink at a local bar and create an artistic 'flatlay' photo from above.
Find a charming street corner where a classic residential building meets a modern cafe or shop and capture the blend.
Inside the market, photograph a stall with a colorful and abundant display of fresh fruits, vegetables, or seafood.
Find a bar famous for its patatas bravas and take a mouth-watering close-up of the dish.
Capture the effortlessly cool and casual style of the locals enjoying a weekend afternoon in the neighborhood.
Photograph an old, traditional shop sign—for a bakery, a hardware store, or a bodega.
Find the preserved Roman ruins visible in the basement level of the Mercat de Sant Antoni and take a picture.
Step off the main drags and capture the peace and quiet of a residential side street.
Find a bicycle parked outside a popular brunch spot and take a photo that sums up the weekend vibe.
Photograph a tree-lined street or a small plaza that provides a touch of nature to the urban landscape.
Take a photo of your drink with the lively street scene of Sant Antoni blurred in the background.
Take a selfie on the beach with the iconic W Hotel in the background.
Get lost in the grid of narrow streets and take a photo looking down a lane with laundry hanging from the balconies.
Find a detail that hints at the neighborhood's fishing past—a boat, a net, or an old seaside tavern.
Take a mouth-watering, top-down photo of a seafood paella at a beachfront restaurant.
Take an artistic photo of your feet in the sand, with the Mediterranean waves in the background.
Capture the lively, local atmosphere of the Plaça del Mercat, the heart of the neighborhood.
Take a panoramic photo of the yachts and boats moored in the Port Vell marina.
Photograph the warm colors of the sunset reflecting on the water or the city skyline from the beach.
Capture an action shot of people playing beach volleyball, paddleboarding, or surfing.
Find a traditional, old-school bar ('bodega' or 'tasca') and photograph its authentic facade.
Take a photo of a seagull, trying to frame it with an interesting background like the sea or a building.
Photograph one of the famous public sculptures along the waterfront, like Rebecca Horn's 'Homenatge a la Barceloneta'.
Capture the relaxed vibe of a 'chiringuito' (beach bar), with people enjoying drinks in the sun.
Look up and take a photo of the red cable car (Telefèric del Port) crossing the sky above the port.
Find the old clock tower (Torre del Rellotge) in the fishermen's wharf and take a picture of this historic landmark.
